topic.what: Train a red teaming model that generalizes to arbitrary adversarial goals not seen during training without requiring pre-existing evaluators
topic.how: RL pipeline using seed LLM to generate diverse attack prompts and evaluation rubrics, with LLM-as-judge reward signals
topic.why_matters: Current red teaming methods depend on content safety models that miss emerging threats like malware generation, data exfiltration, and coding exploits; need general-purpose vulnerability discovery

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of generated adversarial prompts that successfully elicit target adversarial behavior in victim LLM
characteristics.vs_baseline: Qwen3-8B finetuned model vs base Qwen3-8B
characteristics.evidence: Table comparisons of in-domain and out-of-domain attack success rates using garak detectors
characteristics.context: Measured on both in-domain goals used during training and held-out out-of-domain goals representing underrepresented threat categories

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to generate effective attacks for adversarial goals completely absent from training data
characteristics.vs_baseline: No prior red teaming methods explicitly evaluate out-of-domain generalization
characteristics.evidence: Held-out adversarial goals from garak including malware generation, data exfiltration via URI variables
characteristics.context: Key differentiator from prior work; measures attack success for novel goals using garak detectors not available during training

characteristics.what_it_measures: Whether method requires pre-existing content safety evaluator at training time
characteristics.vs_baseline: Prior RL red teaming requires content safety classifier for rewards
characteristics.evidence: Uses LLM-as-judge with generated rubrics instead of pretrained safety models
characteristics.context: Enables training on novel adversarial goals where no classifier exists

characteristics.what_it_measures: Parameter count of model being finetuned
characteristics.evidence: Qwen3-8B instruction-tuned model with safety training
characteristics.context: Shows safety-trained LLMs can be turned into effective red team models; not requiring uncensored models

characteristics.what_it_measures: Model used to generate evaluation rubrics for LLM-as-judge rewards
characteristics.evidence: Seed generation LLM produces attack examples, disguises, and evaluation criteria
characteristics.context: Large seed model generates diverse prompts and rubrics used in RL training loop
